skill.name: kegg-database
skill.description: Access KEGG REST API data for academic bioinformatics research. Use it to query pathways, genes, compounds, diseases, drugs, identifier mappings, and drug interaction records.
skill.summary: Query KEGG pathways, genes, compounds, IDs, and drug interactions through documented REST helpers.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the command execution, weak cryptography, IP address, system reconnaissance, and path traversal alerts are false positives from Markdown examples, REST path syntax, identifier formats, and string slicing. The confirmed risks are intentional outbound HTTPS requests to the public KEGG REST API and caller-controlled REST path segments without strict validation. No prompt injection, credential access, persistence, malware behavior, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: Intentional Public KEGG API Network Access
low_findings.description: The helper functions make outbound HTTPS requests to https://rest.kegg.jp. This matches the skill purpose and is not exfiltration, but user queries and biological identifiers are sent to KEGG.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The base URL and urlopen calls are explicit in the script, and the destination is the documented KEGG API. I found no evidence that secrets or local files are sent.

low_findings.title: Caller-Controlled REST Path Segments
low_findings.description: Several functions interpolate database names, organism codes, entry IDs, and options directly into REST paths. This can produce malformed or unintended KEGG API requests if callers pass unexpected slash-delimited input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly formats caller arguments into URLs, with only kegg_find encoding its search query. The fixed KEGG host limits impact, so the issue is input validation rather than SSRF.

low_findings.title: HTTP Requests Have No Explicit Timeout
low_findings.description: The urllib.request.urlopen calls do not set timeouts. Slow or unavailable network responses can block workflows that call these helpers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each network call uses urlopen without a timeout argument. This is a reliability and availability concern, not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous external command, weak cryptography, hardcoded IP, reconnaissance, and path traversal alerts resolve to Markdown backticks, KEGG REST route examples, biological identifier formats, HTTP status codes, and demo string slicing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed these patterns are examples or literal scientific/API notation, not executable shell commands or cryptographic code. No prompt injection phrases were found in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic URL Construction From Caller Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The script builds KEGG REST URLs by interpolating caller-provided identifiers into path components. Callers should validate identifiers before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present across all REST helper functions. The fixed HTTPS base URL and public API purpose reduce security severity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound HTTP Client Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses urllib.request.urlopen to contact an external API. This is expected for the skill, but it should remain transparent to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and limited to KEGG REST API calls. I found no secondary endpoint or hidden exfiltration channel.

content.user_title: Query KEGG Pathway and Identifier Data
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics work often needs reliable access to KEGG pathways, genes, compounds, drugs, and identifier mappings. This skill provides KEGG REST API guidance and Python helpers for academic research workflows.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve KEGG database metadata and organism-specific information.
List pathways, genes, compounds, enzymes, diseases, and drugs from KEGG databases.
Search KEGG entries by keyword, formula, exact mass, or molecular weight range.
Fetch KEGG entries in text, FASTA, MOL, KCF, image, KGML, or pathway JSON formats.
Convert identifiers between KEGG, NCBI Gene, UniProt, PubChem, ChEBI, and related databases.
Link genes, pathways, compounds, enzymes, reactions, and orthology groups for analysis workflows.
limitations: KEGG API access is limited to academic users under KEGG terms.
The helper returns raw KEGG responses and does not provide full statistical enrichment analysis.
Most KEGG calls are limited to ten entries, and image, KGML, and JSON calls use one entry.
Network calls require live access to rest.kegg.jp and do not include retries or timeout settings.

use_cases.title: Map Genes to Pathways
use_cases.description: Find KEGG pathways connected to a gene list and retrieve pathway details for interpretation.

use_cases.title: Convert Biological Identifiers
use_cases.description: Translate KEGG gene or compound identifiers into NCBI, UniProt, PubChem, or ChEBI identifiers.

use_cases.title: Review Drug Interaction Records
use_cases.description: Query KEGG drug entries and drug-drug interaction records for academic pharmacology research.

prompt_templates.title: Find Human Pathways
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the KEGG skill to list human pathways for organism code hsa and summarize the most relevant entries for glycolysis.

prompt_templates.title: Map One Gene
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the KEGG entry for TP53, map it to pathways, and explain what each pathway connection means.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Identifier Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these KEGG human gene identifiers to UniProt and NCBI Gene IDs, then report missing mappings clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Build Pathway Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a KEGG workflow that links genes, compounds, reactions, and pathways for a metabolic pathway analysis project.

output_examples.input: List human glycolysis pathway information from KEGG.
output_examples.output: A concise pathway summary with the human pathway ID, pathway name, related genes, and notes about KEGG entry limits.

output_examples.input: Convert hsa gene IDs to UniProt IDs.
output_examples.output: A mapping table description showing each KEGG gene, its UniProt match when available, and unresolved identifiers.

output_examples.input: Check KEGG drug-drug interactions for selected drug IDs.
output_examples.output: A research-oriented interaction summary with drug IDs, interaction status, and a warning to verify clinical relevance elsewhere.

best_practices: Validate KEGG organism codes and identifier formats before making API calls.
Batch requests within KEGG entry limits and avoid rapid repeated calls.
Cache non-sensitive KEGG results when a workflow repeats the same queries.
anti_patterns: Do not use KEGG API data for commercial workflows without checking KEGG licensing.
Do not treat raw KEGG drug interaction records as clinical advice.
Do not pass unrestricted user text into REST path parameters without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to the public KEGG REST API at rest.kegg.jp for academic research queries.

faq.question: Does it perform pathway enrichment statistics?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves KEGG pathway and gene data that can support a separate enrichment analysis.

faq.question: Can it retrieve pathway images?
faq.answer: Yes. KEGG supports pathway image retrieval for single entries through the get operation.

faq.question: Can it convert identifiers?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents and wraps KEGG conversion endpoints for NCBI, UniProt, PubChem, ChEBI, and related IDs.

faq.question: Is this suitable for commercial use?
faq.answer: The skill states that KEGG API access is for academic users. Commercial users should confirm licensing with KEGG.

faq.question: Does it store credentials or local files?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files do not access environment secrets or write local data.
